poppyReleaseDate.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Release Date
poppyReleaseDate.subtitle: The release page for Chapter 6 is a live status board: official status, recent chapter cadence, and the strongest public timing window right now.
poppyReleaseDate.intro: Mob Entertainment has not published a standalone Chapter 6 announcement page, store page, or trailer yet. The cleanest user-facing read is that Chapter 5 is the current live endpoint, while the recent mainline cadence points to a 2027 target window for the next chapter.

items.label: Official Status
items.title: No Chapter 6 date is posted by Mob Entertainment
items.body: The official Poppy Playtime page currently lists Chapters 1 through 5, with no separate Chapter 6 page, no launch date, and no official title reveal.

items.label: Latest Live Chapter
items.title: Chapter 5: Broken Things launched on February 18, 2026
items.body: Steam lists Poppy Playtime - Chapter 5 as released on Feb 18, 2026. That makes Chapter 5 the current canonical endpoint for users tracking what comes next.

items.label: Recent Cadence
items.title: The series has settled into a near-yearly rhythm
items.body: Chapter 3 released on January 30, 2024, Chapter 4 on January 30, 2025, and Chapter 5 on February 18, 2026. That recent pattern gives the next chapter a natural 2027 lane.

items.label: Working Window
items.title: 2027 is the strongest public target window
items.body: Across current public coverage, the most grounded timing window is 2027, especially after the February 2026 release of Chapter 5 and the annual rhythm of the previous two launches.

poppyTrailer.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Trailer
poppyTrailer.subtitle: There is no official Chapter 6 trailer yet, so the best trailer hub is the final stretch of Chapter 5's official marketing campaign that sets up the next chapter.
poppyTrailer.intro: Until Mob Entertainment starts the Chapter 6 rollout, users mainly want one place to watch the official lead-in material in order: teaser, cinematic setup, Prototype-focused cinematic, and the final gameplay trailer.

items.label: Official Teaser
items.title: Chapter 5 Teaser Trailer
items.body: Released on October 31, 2025. This is the first official signal of the post-Chapter 4 arc and the earliest trailer users should watch before moving into the late-game setup.

items.label: Cinematic Setup
items.title: Welcome Home
items.body: Released on December 5, 2025. This cinematic trailer follows Poppy after the ending of Chapter 4 and bridges directly into the emotional and narrative pressure that drives Chapter 5.

items.label: Villain Focus
items.title: The Prototype
items.body: Released on January 10, 2026. This cinematic trailer puts Experiment 1006 at the center, reveals Chapter 5's title and release date, and is the clearest official setup for the final conflict ahead.

items.label: Gameplay Reveal
items.title: Official Game Trailer
items.body: Released on February 11, 2026. This is the final pre-launch trailer for Broken Things and the best gameplay-heavy watch for understanding what Chapter 6 will inherit from Chapter 5.

poppyStoryTheories.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Story Theories
poppyStoryTheories.subtitle: The strongest Chapter 6 theories are not random guesses. They come straight from the Chapter 5 ending: Poppy is taken, Sawyer returns through the Backup, and the Prototype is still standing.

items.title: Chapter 6 is set up as a rescue mission for Poppy
items.summary: The ending leaves Poppy in the Prototype's hands, which gives the next chapter a direct objective instead of another broad descent into the factory.
points: The Prototype leaves Chapter 5 with Poppy, making her recovery the clearest next-step goal.
This turns the next chapter into an active pursuit instead of a fresh mystery reset.
A rescue structure also fits the series' current push toward endgame confrontation rather than slow-burn setup.

items.title: Harley Sawyer is positioned as the biggest wild card in the chapter
items.summary: The Backup reveal does more than revive an old villain. It creates a power struggle where Sawyer may have to help the player against the Prototype before turning dangerous again.
points: The Master Backup ends with Harley Sawyer returning through the data system.
He has direct relevance to the experiments, procedures, and factory systems that matter most now.
That makes him the cleanest route to an uneasy ally-to-rival arc in the next chapter.

items.title: The Prototype remains the final central threat
items.summary: Chapter 5 gives players a much clearer look at Experiment 1006, but it does not finish him. Instead, it sharpens the path toward a larger endgame clash.
points: The Prototype survives the train sequence and still controls the board by the end of Broken Things.
His presence escalates from hidden mastermind to direct physical threat.
Everything now points toward Chapter 6 pushing the series into a true final-stage confrontation.

items.title: Unresolved allies and bodies keep the next chapter emotionally loaded
items.summary: Chapter 5 refuses to close the book on Huggy Wuggy, Kissy Missy, and the surviving allies underground, which gives Chapter 6 room to combine rescue, reunion, and betrayal.
points: Huggy Wuggy and Kissy Missy are attacked, but their final status is left open.
Giblet survives the chapter and is still embedded in the fight against the Prototype.
That unresolved group dynamic gives Chapter 6 more than one thread to pay off at once.

poppyEndingSetup.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Ending Setup
poppyEndingSetup.subtitle: Chapter 5 does not end with closure. It stacks four major turns in a row: the Wuggy reunion is shattered, the train escape fails, Poppy is taken, and Harley Sawyer comes back through the Backup.

items.title: The reunion collapses as soon as the Prototype returns
items.summary: Chapter 5 briefly reunites Huggy Wuggy and Kissy Missy, then immediately turns that into a fresh disaster when the Prototype crashes the moment.
points: The Prototype interrupts the reunion and attacks both of them.
Their condition is left unresolved rather than fully closed off.
That choice preserves them as major emotional stakes for the next chapter.

items.title: The train escape ends with a stab, a Poppy Gel tank, and a revival
items.summary: The player boards a train to escape, but the Prototype keeps pace, survives the chaos, and lands a final blow before the story pivots again.
points: The Prototype catches the player after the train sequence and stabs them through the chest.
The body is dumped into Poppy Gel instead of being left behind.
Giblet revives the player, pushing the chapter past the fake-out ending and into its final reveal.

items.title: Poppy is taken, which gives the next chapter its clearest objective
items.summary: By the time the player is back on their feet, the Prototype has already left with Poppy.
points: The player survives, but the rescue fails in the short term.
Poppy's removal from the scene turns the ending into a direct setup for pursuit.
This is the cleanest bridge from Broken Things into a Chapter 6 rescue arc.

items.title: The Master Backup ends by restoring Harley Sawyer
items.summary: The final reveal is not a file dump or a weapon blueprint. It is Harley Sawyer himself, returned through the Backup system.
points: The chapter's search for the Backup ends in the Data Storage room.
Activating it reveals Harley Sawyer as the stored intelligence behind the Backup.
That one reveal reframes Chapter 6 around three forces at once: the player, Sawyer, and the Prototype.

poppyCharacters.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Characters
poppyCharacters.subtitle: Chapter 5 leaves a tighter, more important cast around the Player. These allies, survivors, and carryover threats are the names most likely to shape the next chapter.
poppyCharacters.intro: The ending of Broken Things narrows the field around a handful of characters who either escape with the Player, help drive the anti-Prototype plan, or return at the exact moment the story needs a new power player.

items.name: Poppy
items.summary: Poppy remains the emotional center of the series and one of the Player's most important guides. In Chapter 5, she is freed, injured by the Prototype during the tea-party confrontation, and brought back into the surviving group.
details: Officially presented as Playtime Co.'s breakout doll with a voice box
Freed during the late Chapter 5 escape sequence
Reunites with the group after the Prototype damages her

items.name: Kissy Missy
items.summary: Kissy Missy is one of the last major friendly toys still moving with the Player's side. She attacks the Prototype at Lily Lovebraids' tea party, creating the opening that lets the escape continue.
details: Listed as a Chapter 5 deuteragonist
Breaks the Prototype's immediate control of the tea-party scene
Reappears near the tram station during the endgame run

items.name: Giblet
items.summary: Giblet is one of Chapter 5's most important new helpers. He is part of the small group actively working against the Prototype and is directly tied to the plan around the negation formula backup.
details: Listed as a Chapter 5 deuteragonist
Explains that the backup contains the Prototype's weakness
Pushes the Player toward the tram and the next objective

items.name: Chum Chompkins
items.summary: Chum Chompkins works alongside Giblet in Chapter 5 and becomes part of the Player's narrow support network. He begins distrustful, but quickly becomes part of the operation moving the Player deeper through the Labs.
details: Listed as a Chapter 5 deuteragonist
Helps protect the Player when the Prototype approaches
Tied to the same anti-Prototype plan as Giblet

items.name: Harley Sawyer
items.summary: Harley Sawyer shifts from background history into direct Chapter 6 relevance at the end of Chapter 5. The backup the Player is sent to recover turns out to be Harley Sawyer himself, putting him back at the center of the story.
details: Appears in Chapter 5 as a plot-twist comeback
Known as the architect of the Bigger Bodies Initiative
Now positioned as a major carryover character for the next chapter

items.name: The Prototype
items.summary: Even inside a character board, the Prototype cannot be separated from the Chapter 6 setup. Chapter 5 gives him a direct face-to-face confrontation with Poppy and the Player, making him the figure every surviving character is reacting to.
details: Official bio describes him as intelligent, cunning, and crafty
Damages Poppy during the tea-party confrontation
Stalks the Player through the endgame route toward the tram

poppyVillains.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Villains
poppyVillains.subtitle: Broken Things narrows the next chapter's danger map around a few major names. This module shows the antagonists and wildcard forces carrying the most story weight into the next phase.
poppyVillains.intro: Players searching for the next villain usually want more than a name. They want to know who is driving the story, who is doing the physical hunting, and who might destabilize the balance between the Player and the Prototype.

items.name: The Prototype
items.summary: The Prototype is the main antagonist of Poppy Playtime and the dominant force behind the end of Chapter 5. He directly confronts Poppy, damages her, stalks the Player, and remains the center of the factory's power structure.
details: Also known as Experiment 1006
Described as cunning, intelligent, and violent
Orchestrated the Hour of Joy and drives the late-game pressure in Broken Things

items.name: Harley Sawyer
items.summary: Harley Sawyer matters because he combines human-era experiment history with a live return to the story. As the mind behind the Bigger Bodies Initiative and the ending reveal behind the backup, he enters Chapter 6 as a destabilizing force.
details: Known to most characters as The Doctor
Responsible for the Bigger Bodies Initiative
Reintroduced as the backup recovered at the end of Chapter 5

items.name: Huggy Wuggy
items.summary: Huggy Wuggy remains one of the series' most dangerous chase threats. Chapter 5 keeps him active as the factory's pursuing force, including a major ambush near the tram-side endgame stretch.
details: Serves as Playtime's security-system pursuer in Chapter 5
Keeps direct physical pressure on the Player
Helps keep the finale dangerous even before the last confrontation peaks

poppyPrototype.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 The Prototype
poppyPrototype.subtitle: The Prototype is the one name every Chapter 6 theory circles back to. This module gives a clean read on his identity, his actions, and why the next chapter cannot move forward without him.
poppyPrototype.intro: Prototype searches sit at the center of late-stage Poppy Playtime interest because they connect lore, motive, and future story direction in one place. Instead of scattering across old chapter summaries, this module organizes the core facts that matter most after Broken Things.

items.title: Identity
items.content: The Prototype is Experiment 1006 and the main antagonist of Poppy Playtime. Official character material describes him as intelligent, cunning, and crafty, while the lore pages present him as a feared and respected force inside the factory.

items.title: Series Role
items.content: His presence runs across the full story arc. He is referenced as early as Chapter 1, physically tied to later chapter events, revealed as the main antagonist of Broken Things, and treated as the central architect behind the factory's long-running horrors.

items.title: Chapter 5 Actions
items.content: Broken Things moves him from background menace to direct confrontation. At Lily Lovebraids' tea party, he faces Poppy and the Player head-on, says he sent the orphans to a Better Place, damages Poppy, and continues stalking the Player through the endgame route.

items.title: Why He Matters for Chapter 6
items.content: Chapter 5 ends with the anti-Prototype plan still unresolved, which leaves him as the pressure point for every remaining character. Poppy, Giblet, Kissy Missy, and Harley Sawyer are all now defined by how they oppose, understand, or survive him.

poppyHarleySawyer.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Harley Sawyer
poppyHarleySawyer.subtitle: Harley Sawyer is no longer just a name buried in experiment lore. Chapter 5 pushes him back into the main story and turns him into one of the most important human-linked figures in the next chapter.
poppyHarleySawyer.intro: Users searching Harley Sawyer usually want three things at once: who he was, what he built, and why his return changes the stakes. This module answers all three in one place.

items.title: Who Harley Sawyer Is
items.content: Harley Sawyer is the Head of Special Projects and the figure most characters know as The Doctor. Official material describes him as the originator of the Bigger Bodies Initiative and a ruthless, egotistical scientist tied to the factory's darkest work.

items.title: The Doctor
items.content: The official character bio presents him as a mind trapped in wires and terminals after the same kind of experimentation he inflicted on others. That transformation is what turns Harley Sawyer from a corporate-era scientist into one of the series' most disturbing recurring figures.

items.title: Bigger Bodies Initiative
items.content: Harley Sawyer's biggest story function is simple: he built the program that converted orphans into living toys. That makes him one of the clearest links between Playtime Co.'s human leadership, the experiment system, and the monsters still shaping the story.

items.title: Why Chapter 5 Changes Everything
items.content: At the end of Chapter 5, the backup the Player is sent to recover is revealed to be Harley Sawyer. That twist pulls him out of archived history and places him directly into the next phase of the conflict around the Prototype.

poppyWalkthrough.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Chapter 5 Walkthrough
poppyWalkthrough.subtitle: This is the fastest clean route through Broken Things if you want to catch up on the story, key upgrades, and the biggest puzzle gates before Chapter 6.
poppyWalkthrough.intro: Chapter 5 runs from the opening Huggy Wuggy escape through the Pressure Hand unlock, the memory puzzles, Lily Lovebraids' dollhouse sequence, and the Reanimation finale. Use this path to finish the chapter and arrive at the ending with the important story beats fresh in mind.

items.objective: Escape Huggy Wuggy and reach the lower labs
items.what_to_do: Wait for the rear door to open, sprint through the vent route, crouch under the conveyor hazards, bend the metal panel on the left, and hit the blue scanner before the red-blue gate.
items.result: You survive the opening chase and enter the first exploration-heavy lab section.

items.objective: Navigate the dark hallways and get the key
items.what_to_do: Follow the blacklight arrows, clear the side rooms, grab the Sisters audio log, then move through the stair room and collect the Recycling & Reclamation Key before regrouping with Giblet.
items.result: This opens the route into the Boilers and sets up the first safer stretch of the chapter.

items.objective: Recover your GrabPack and unlock the Pressure Hand
items.what_to_do: Climb Bron the Dinosaur to reclaim the GrabPack, drag the chained platform left to cross into the Main Office, use the blacklight on the whiteboard, flip it, and enter 0859 to open the Pressure Hand locker.
items.result: You gain the chapter's first mandatory upgrade and unlock vent breaking, charged pushes, and several later puzzles.

items.objective: Collect the three memories and survive the Huggy Wuggy pressure sequence
items.what_to_do: Use the machine room memory chip, then complete the Conditioning projector chain to reach the second memory, clear the red smoke by turning valves in the order Triangle, Star, Circle, Hexagon for the third memory route, and finish all three Huggy memory scenes.
items.result: You open the machine door, advance the main story, and clear one of the chapter's most important lore gates.

items.section: Sweet Street / The Dollhouse / Tea Time
items.objective: Finish Lily Lovebraids' tasks and escape her mansion
items.what_to_do: Cross Sweet Street, then in the Dollhouse retrieve Lily's three toys: the car toy by powering the garage, the piano toy by launching it and playing the music-sheet order, and the shower toy by using the balcony fire route. After the tea-party reveal, run the chase and keep moving until Lily is defeated.
items.result: The Lily Lovebraids arc ends and the story turns toward Reanimation and the final backup objective.

items.section: Reanimation / The End of the Line
items.objective: Find the backup, clear Surgery and Grand Stitchery, then finish the train finale
items.what_to_do: Solve the randomized lab code, use 231 for the observation corridor, enter Surgery 2 for the red VHS locker code 728, complete all four Grand Stitchery power tasks, reach the tram, pull yourself onto the train with the magnetic pads, and push through to the override terminal.
items.result: You complete Chapter 5 and reach the ending sequence that directly sets up the next chapter.

poppyPuzzleCodes.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Puzzle Codes
poppyPuzzleCodes.subtitle: Chapter 5 codes, passwords, and puzzle sequences pulled into one fast-scan block for players who want immediate solutions.
poppyPuzzleCodes.intro: Broken Things uses number pads, symbol orders, hidden UV clues, and a few late-game doors that are easy to forget after a death or reload. This list covers the most common blockers players hit in Chapter 5.

items.label: Pressure Hand Locker
items.location: Main Office after crossing from the Boilers area
items.hint: Use the blacklight on the whiteboard, flip it over, and read the hidden digits on the back.

items.label: Master Backup Files Locker
items.use: Opens the locker holding the Master Backup files
items.hint: Use the UV clue on the whiteboard, inspect the SP shelf through the window, then confirm the marked container from the metal walkway.

items.label: Red Smoke Valve Puzzle
items.use: Clears the smoke so you can continue the third memory path
items.hint: Read the blacklight shapes on the wall upstairs, then turn the valves in that order before flipping the lever.

items.label: Emotion Keypad Door
items.code: Happy → Happy → Disgust → Sad → Fear → Happy
items.location: Conditioning corridor with the projector rooms
items.use: Opens the optional hallway with the Experiment 1170 VHS tape
items.hint: The face order is shown during the final Huggy memory breakout.

items.label: BLANKY Blankz Locker
items.location: Projector arena during the Huggy distraction sequence
items.hint: The digits are scattered through the safe rooms around the arena perimeter.

items.label: Lily's Piano Puzzle
items.location: Upstairs music room in Lily Lovebraids' mansion
items.use: Opens the piano and releases one tea-party toy
items.hint: Use the Pressure Hand to launch the toy into the piano first, then play the order shown on the nearby sheet music.

items.label: Backup Red Door
items.location: Late Reanimation lab room before the Surgery corridor
items.use: Opens the route toward the Surgical and Observation areas
items.hint: The four digits are hidden on separate paper clues in the same room.

items.label: Surgery Observation Corridor
items.location: Upper corridor above the Grand Stitchery
items.use: Opens the locked observation doors leading into the surgery suites
items.hint: Reveal the number on the wall between the doors with the blacklight.

items.label: Red VHS Tape Cabinet
items.use: Unlocks the final red VHS tape cabinet
items.hint: Break into Surgery 2, scan the wall with the blacklight, then enter the locker code.

items.label: Memories Machine Door
items.location: Locked door behind the memory machine
items.use: Opens the route after all three Huggy Wuggy memories
items.hint: Complete all three memories to receive two digits per sequence on your own run.

poppyPressureHand.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Pressure Hand Guide
poppyPressureHand.subtitle: The Pressure Hand is the first major GrabPack upgrade in Chapter 5 and it is mandatory for progression. This guide helps you unlock it quickly and understand where it matters afterward.
poppyPressureHand.intro: Players first encounter the Pressure Hand in the early lab route after reclaiming the GrabPack. Once unlocked, it becomes the chapter's core tool for breaking vents, shattering barriers, moving carts, powering puzzle setups, and opening later routes in Testing, the Dollhouse, and Reanimation.

items.heading: Reclaim the GrabPack
items.action: Go to the Boilers area, walk up Bron the Dinosaur's tail, jump onto the nearby blue box, then leap to the overhanging blue pipe and follow the catwalk to the GrabPack.
items.result: You get the base tool required to reach the Main Office and the Pressure Hand locker.

items.heading: Build the bridge into the Main Office
items.action: Use the GrabPack on the chained platform to your right and drag it all the way left so it becomes a makeshift bridge across the gap.
items.result: This takes you into the Main Office where the Pressure Hand locker puzzle begins.

items.heading: Reveal the locker clue
items.action: Find the whiteboard, switch on the blacklight, follow the hidden arrow, and flip the board over to expose the concealed digits.
items.result: The code shown on the board is 0859.

items.heading: Open the locker and equip the upgrade
items.action: Enter 0859 on the locker keypad and collect the Pressure Hand.
items.result: Your GrabPack now gains a charged blast that can open new routes and solve several mandatory puzzles.

items.heading: Use the charge correctly
items.action: Attach the hand to a surface, hold the charge, then release to fire a pressurized blast. A longer charge produces a stronger impact.
items.result: You can now break vents, glass, icy or blocked surfaces, and push objects or buttons that regular hands cannot move.

items.heading: Watch for the first important uses
items.action: Use the Pressure Hand to break the vent after the locker, move heavy carts in Research & Development, solve Dollhouse toy puzzles, break into Surgery 2, and finish the Grand Stitchery pressure setup.
items.result: These are the main story checkpoints where the upgrade becomes part of the critical path.

poppyTapeLocations.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Tape Locations
poppyTapeLocations.subtitle: Chapter 5 has three VHS tapes and ten audio logs — several are easy to miss because the game locks players out of earlier rooms after autosaves.
poppyTapeLocations.intro: Lore players searching for Chapter 6 clues often go back to Broken Things to replay tapes, relisten to logs, and reconnect character reveals with the late-game ending. This list surfaces the most important VHS pickups and easiest-to-miss audio logs.

items.name: Experiment 1170
items.location: On the floor behind the emotion keypad door in the corridor with the eight conditioning rooms.
items.unlock_or_route: Enter Happy → Happy → Disgust → Sad → Fear → Happy to open the hallway.
items.playback: Use the cyan TV in the Conditioning Room at the end of the corridor.
items.missable_tip: Grab it during the three-memories section before pushing too far ahead.

items.name: Three P's of Diorama Safety
items.location: On the climbing-wall ramp in the playground at the first stop of the ice-cream truck ride.
items.unlock_or_route: Climb the frame from the back side of the playground and pick it up before leaving the area.
items.playback: Use the yellow TV in the Toy Treehouse above the playground.
items.missable_tip: Do this before you leave the playground route and continue through Sweet Street.

items.name: How to Maintain Your–(Illegible)
items.location: Inside the locked locker in Surgery 2.
items.unlock_or_route: Break both cracked windows, route power into Surgery 2, then enter 728 on the locker.
items.playback: Use the VCR in nearby Surgery 1.
items.missable_tip: Pick it up before finishing Grand Stitchery and moving into the final chapter stretch.

items.name: Sisters
items.location: Through the left-side door near the bathroom and tent detour on the blacklight-arrow route.
items.unlock_or_route: Crawl under the tent for the side path, then enter the left door to collect the log before taking the stairs-room route to the key.
items.playback: Listen from the inventory audio log menu.
items.missable_tip: Easy to skip if you go straight for the key path.

items.name: Security Log - 08081995-16
items.location: Inside the locked room reached through the fallen vent after leaving Giblet's hideout.
items.unlock_or_route: Drop into the large room, climb into the fallen vent, crawl through, and collect the cassette before leaving the room.
items.playback: Listen from the inventory audio log menu.
items.missable_tip: Pick it up before following the obvious unlocked-door route forward.

items.name: The Case
items.location: In the right corner of the middle walkway reached after the first Pressure Hand cart push.
items.unlock_or_route: Use the Pressure Hand to move the cart, jump to the middle walkway for the detour, grab the cassette, then return and continue the main path.
items.playback: Listen from the inventory audio log menu.
items.missable_tip: Do the detour before pushing the cart all the way to the back vent.

poppyCollectiblesGuide.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Collectibles Guide
poppyCollectiblesGuide.subtitle: Use the latest full-completion pattern from the series to prep your next perfect run. Chapter 5 sets the current benchmark with five pickup categories and 74 total trackable items.
poppyCollectiblesGuide.intro: For completionists, the most useful way to frame Chapter 6 is through the collectible structure already established in Chapter 5: main collectibles, cardboard cutouts, audio tapes, VHS tapes, and notes. This checklist highlights the biggest categories, the most valuable chase items, and the zones with the heaviest pickup density.

items.label: Full chapter tracker
items.summary: The current series benchmark for a full-clear collection run.

items.label: Main collectibles
items.summary: The core collectibles tab mixes character items, rare gold variants, and lore objects.

items.label: Golden set
items.summary: The cleanest rare-item subset to spotlight in a premium checklist.

items.label: Wrongside Outimals
items.summary: The largest named collectible family in the current chapter structure.

items.label: Cardboard cutouts
items.summary: Cutouts unlock voice lines and work well as fast visual checklist items.

items.label: Audio and VHS media
entries: VHS: Three P's of Diorama Safety
items.summary: Small in number but high in story value — ideal for a lore-focused sub-checklist.

items.label: Best collection zones
entries: The Doll House — 4 collectibles and 6 notes
items.summary: These sections carry the highest concentration of missable chapter-wide pickups.

poppyNotesDocuments.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Notes and Documents
poppyNotesDocuments.subtitle: The current note trail in the series is already deep enough to support a dedicated lore archive. Chapter 5 alone contains 35 readable notes and documents spread across nine major sections.
poppyNotesDocuments.intro: Players searching Chapter 6 theories usually want one thing first: the paper trail. This table organizes the latest chapter's document structure by area, note count, and standout files so readers can catch up on the most important written evidence.

items.key_documents: An Intercepted Communique; Shipping Manifest – July '96; Inventory – September '95
items.focus: Opening logistics, shipments, and the first hard clues from the factory floor

items.key_documents: Work Procedures – Recycling & Reclamation; Outimal Note; Wrongside Outimal Branding Note
items.focus: Process rules, branding language, and early Wrongside Outimal context

items.key_documents: Giblet's Checklist; The Better Press, Issue 14
items.focus: Local operations and character-facing material tied to Giblet

items.key_documents: Lab Report: Medium Growth & Plantlife; GrabPack Hands Rejection; Negation Compound Presentation; To Whomever Finds This
items.focus: The biggest lore cluster — experimentation, backups, compounds, and internal fallout

items.key_documents: Conditioning Observations; Observation: Lily; Letter to Leith #2
items.focus: Behavioral tracking and Lily-focused observation material

items.key_documents: Experiment 1468 Proposal; Music Sheet – Novice; Nursery Damage; Lily Journal
items.focus: Lily's story layer, music-based discovery, and the strongest character-centric documents

items.key_documents: Dr. Akins Personal Letter 3; Experiment Notes
items.focus: Late-stage medical and experiment-heavy reading toward the final stretch
Endgame reading with strong payoff for theory and ending discussion

poppyPlatforms.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Platforms
poppyPlatforms.subtitle: The newest chapter storefront pattern is PC-first. Chapter 5 is live on Steam and Epic Games Store, while earlier official play-now pages confirm the franchise's broader console and mobile footprint.
poppyPlatforms.intro: For a Chapter 6 availability tracker, lead with the platforms already carrying the newest content, then keep the wider franchise platforms in a clear second-wave tracker. That matches how Poppy Playtime is currently surfaced across official platform pages and store listings.

poppyPriceDlc.title: Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 Price and DLC
poppyPriceDlc.subtitle: The live storefront model is straightforward: the base game is free and includes Chapter 1, while later chapters are sold separately as paid DLC or add-ons. The current newest-chapter benchmark is $19.99.
poppyPriceDlc.intro: The clearest way to explain Chapter 6 pricing is to show the structure players already see on live storefronts today. On both Steam and Epic, Chapter 1 sits inside the base game, while Chapters 2 through 5 are listed as separate paid content.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Poppy Playtime Chapter 6

questions.question: When is Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 coming out?
questions.answer: No official release date has been announced by Mob Entertainment. Based on the series' recent yearly cadence—Chapter 3 in January 2024, Chapter 4 in January 2025, Chapter 5 in February 2026—the strongest timing window for Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 is 2027.

questions.question: Is there an official Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 trailer?
questions.answer: No official Chapter 6 trailer has been released yet. The most recent official material is the Chapter 5 marketing campaign, including the gameplay trailer released on February 11, 2026, which sets up the story direction for the next chapter.

questions.question: What happened at the end of Poppy Playtime Chapter 5?
questions.answer: Poppy Playtime Chapter 5 ends with the Prototype taking Poppy, the player surviving a stabbing through the Poppy Gel revival, and Harley Sawyer revealed as the Backup's stored intelligence. These four events directly set up Chapter 6's storyline.

questions.question: Who is the main villain in Poppy Playtime Chapter 6?
questions.answer: The Prototype remains the central threat heading into Poppy Playtime Chapter 6. He survives the Chapter 5 train sequence and leaves with Poppy. Harley Sawyer's return through the Backup also positions him as a major new force in the next chapter.

questions.question: What platforms will Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 be on?
questions.answer: Based on the Chapter 5 model, Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 is expected to launch on PC through Steam and Epic Games first. Console availability remains unconfirmed until Mob Entertainment makes an official announcement.

questions.question: How many chapters of Poppy Playtime are there?
questions.answer: As of February 2026, there are five released chapters: Chapter 1 (A Tight Squeeze), Chapter 2 (Fly in a Web), Chapter 3 (Deep Sleep), Chapter 4 (The Poppy Chapter), and Chapter 5 (Broken Things). Poppy Playtime Chapter 6 has not yet been officially announced.
